places.name: Rock Garden
places.description: A surreal fantasy world built entirely from urban and industrial waste — thousands of sculptures made from broken bangles, tiles, and discarded items spread across 40 acres of winding pathways and waterfalls.
places.history: Secretly created by road inspector Nek Chand starting in 1957 using recycled waste. Discovered in 1975 and turned into a public attraction. Now has over 5,000 sculptures.
places.famousFor: World-famous recycled art sculpture garden.

places.name: Sukhna Lake
places.description: A serene man-made lake nestled at the foothills of the Shivalik range, where golden sunsets paint the water while joggers, boaters, and artists gather along its tranquil promenade.
places.history: Created in 1958 by damming the Sukhna Choe stream as part of Le Corbusier's city plan. Declared a protected wetland and bird sanctuary.
places.famousFor: Sunset views, boating, birdwatching.

places.name: Zakir Hussain Rose Garden
places.description: Asia's largest rose garden spread across 30 acres, bursting with over 1,600 species of roses in a rainbow of colors, alongside medicinal herb sections and ornamental trees.
places.history: Established in 1967 and named after India's third President, Zakir Hussain. Hosts the famous annual Rose Festival every February-March.
places.famousFor: Asia's largest rose garden, 1,600+ rose species.
places.bestTime: February to March (Rose Festival), October to March

places.name: Capitol Complex
places.description: Le Corbusier's architectural masterpiece and UNESCO World Heritage Site — the seat of government featuring the iconic High Court, Secretariat, and Legislative Assembly buildings.
places.history: Designed by Swiss-French architect Le Corbusier in the 1950s. Inscribed as UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2016 as an outstanding example of modern architecture.
places.famousFor: UNESCO World Heritage Site, Le Corbusier's modernism.

places.name: Open Hand Monument
places.description: The official emblem of Chandigarh — a 26-metre-high metal sculpture that rotates with the wind, symbolizing "the hand to give and the hand to take; peace and prosperity, and the unity of mankind."
places.history: Designed by Le Corbusier as the symbol of his utopian city. The 26-metre revolving monument was completed in 1985 and stands in the Capitol Complex plaza.
places.famousFor: Chandigarh's official emblem, Le Corbusier's iconic design.

places.name: Sector 17 Plaza
places.description: The vibrant pedestrian-only commercial hub of Chandigarh — a Le Corbusier-designed open plaza with interconnected walkways, fountains, shops, restaurants, and a buzzing nightlife scene.
places.history: Designed as the city's central business district in Le Corbusier's original masterplan. The pedestrianized plaza was one of India's first car-free shopping zones.
places.famousFor: Main commercial hub, pedestrian plaza, nightlife, and shopping.

places.name: High Court Building
places.description: A monumental Le Corbusier masterpiece — the Punjab and Haryana High Court features dramatic pilotis, a rooftop reflecting pool, and the architect's signature brise-soleil sun-breakers.
places.history: Completed in 1956 as one of the first buildings of the Capitol Complex. Its dramatic entrance with painted pylons is considered one of Le Corbusier's finest judicial buildings.
places.famousFor: Le Corbusier's judicial architecture, painted pylons.

places.name: Secretariat Building
places.description: The administrative heart of Chandigarh — an eight-storey Le Corbusier building housing government offices, featuring his trademark Modulor proportioning system and rooftop garden.
places.history: Completed in 1958 as the tallest building in the Capitol Complex. Houses the administrative offices of both Punjab and Haryana state governments.
places.famousFor: Le Corbusier's Modulor system, seat of dual-state government.

places.name: Legislative Assembly Building
places.description: The most dramatic of Le Corbusier's Capitol buildings — crowned by a hyperbolic paraboloid cooling tower that has become an icon of modern architecture worldwide.
places.history: Completed in 1964 as the last of the three major Capitol Complex buildings. Its distinctive cooling tower and portico are among the most photographed architectural elements in India.
places.famousFor: Iconic cooling tower, Le Corbusier's finest parliament building.

places.name: Le Corbusier Centre
places.description: A museum dedicated to the visionary architect who designed Chandigarh, housed in his former office building — showcasing rare sketches, furniture, and the city's planning history.
places.history: Located in the building that served as Le Corbusier's office during the city's construction in the 1950s. Contains original drawings and correspondence.
places.famousFor: Le Corbusier's original sketches, city planning documents, and furniture.

places.name: Government Museum & Art Gallery
places.description: A Le Corbusier-designed museum housing an extraordinary collection of Gandhara sculptures, Pahari miniature paintings, and contemporary Indian art in a modernist setting.
places.history: Established in 1947 and designed by Le Corbusier. Houses one of the finest collections of Gandharan Buddhist art outside Afghanistan and Pakistan.
places.famousFor: Gandhara sculptures, Pahari paintings, and Le Corbusier architecture.

places.name: International Dolls Museum
places.description: A charming museum housing a vibrant collection of dolls and puppets from over 25 countries, showcasing diverse cultures, costumes, and traditions from around the world.
places.history: Established in the 1980s, the museum features collections donated by various embassies and cultural organizations, representing global diversity.
places.famousFor: Dolls from 25+ countries and cultural costumes.

places.name: Museum of Evolution of Life
places.description: A fascinating natural history museum tracing the evolution of life on Earth through fossils, dioramas, and interactive exhibits — from prehistoric marine creatures to modern mammals.
places.history: Located in the museum complex of Sector 10, this museum showcases geological and paleontological specimens collected from across India, with a focus on the Siwalik fossil beds.
places.famousFor: Siwalik fossils, evolution exhibits, and natural history dioramas.

places.name: Japanese Garden
places.description: A tranquil zen-inspired garden featuring Japanese pagodas, a serene water body with koi, bamboo groves, and wooden bridges — a peaceful escape in the heart of the city.
places.history: Spread over 13 acres, this garden was designed inspired by traditional Japanese landscape aesthetics and serves as a meditation and relaxation space.
places.famousFor: Japanese pagodas, zen garden, and koi pond.

places.name: Garden of Fragrance
places.description: A sensory garden designed specifically for the visually impaired, featuring night-blooming jasmine, fragrant herbs, and aromatic plants that fill the air with natural perfumes.
places.history: Part of the Leisure Valley chain, this unique garden was created to provide an olfactory experience, with plants labeled in Braille for visually impaired visitors.
places.famousFor: Fragrant plants, Braille labels, and sensory garden design.

places.name: Terraced Garden
places.description: A beautifully landscaped multi-level garden with illuminated musical fountains that come alive every evening with a dazzling light and water show.
places.history: Part of the Leisure Valley chain, the Terraced Garden features seasonal flower displays and was developed as a major evening attraction for the city.
places.famousFor: Musical fountain show, evening illumination, and seasonal flowers.

places.name: Butterfly Park
places.description: A colorful garden dedicated to attracting and conserving butterflies, with host plants, nectar gardens, and educational exhibits about lepidoptera.
places.history: Developed as part of Chandigarh's green belt initiative to promote butterfly conservation and environmental awareness among visitors.
places.famousFor: Butterfly conservation, host plants, and nature education.

places.name: Bougainvillea Garden
places.description: A vibrant garden showcasing over 65 varieties of bougainvillea in a riot of magenta, orange, purple, and white — cascading over trellises and pergolas.
places.history: Part of Chandigarh's extensive garden network in the Leisure Valley. Features one of the largest collections of bougainvillea varieties in North India.
places.famousFor: 65+ bougainvillea varieties, colorful displays.

places.name: Botanical Garden
places.description: A sprawling 176-acre botanical paradise housing diverse plant species, a medicinal plants section, and a small hillock offering panoramic views of the city.
places.history: Established to preserve and showcase the botanical diversity of the region, featuring both native and exotic plant species in well-maintained themed sections.
places.famousFor: Diverse plant collections, medicinal herbs, and panoramic views.

places.name: Chandigarh Bird Park
places.description: A modern aviary and bird park featuring walk-through enclosures with exotic and indigenous bird species, interactive exhibits, and conservation education programs.
places.history: Opened in 2019, the park features over 70 species of birds in naturalistic habitats, with the aim of promoting avian conservation and providing an educational experience.
places.famousFor: Walk-through aviary, 70+ bird species, conservation education.

places.name: Shanti Kunj
places.description: A tranquil meditation garden adjacent to the Rose Garden, featuring manicured lawns, shaded walkways, and quiet seating areas designed for contemplation.
places.history: Developed as a peaceful retreat within the Leisure Valley, Shanti Kunj provides a serene space for yoga, meditation, and quiet walks.
places.famousFor: Meditation space, peaceful ambiance, proximity to Rose Garden.

places.name: Children Traffic Park
places.description: An educational park with miniature roads, traffic signals, and kid-sized vehicles where children learn road safety through hands-on play.
places.history: One of Chandigarh's unique family attractions, the park was designed to educate children about traffic rules and road safety in an engaging, interactive environment.
places.famousFor: Traffic education for children, miniature roads, and kid-sized vehicles.

places.name: Chandigarh Haat
places.description: An open-air crafts market showcasing traditional Punjabi and Haryanvi handicrafts, street food, and cultural performances in a vibrant bazaar-style setting.
places.history: Established to promote regional arts and crafts, Chandigarh Haat serves as a cultural marketplace where artisans from across the region display and sell their work.
places.famousFor: Regional handicrafts, Punjabi street food, and cultural events.

places.name: Leisure Valley
places.description: An 8-km green belt running through the heart of Chandigarh, linking themed gardens — from bougainvillea to hibiscus — creating a continuous urban parkland unlike any other Indian city.
places.history: Part of Le Corbusier's original city masterplan, designed as a continuous green lung stretching across the city. Houses multiple themed gardens and art installations.
places.famousFor: City-wide green corridor, themed gardens, and public art.

places.name: Garden of Silence
places.description: A serene meditation garden centered around a majestic seated Buddha statue, where silence and tranquility reign — designed as a retreat from the bustle of modern city life.
places.history: Created as a contemplation space within Chandigarh's green belt, featuring a large Buddha statue and landscaped areas designed for quiet reflection.
places.famousFor: Buddha statue, meditation space, and peaceful ambiance.

places.name: Topiary Park
places.description: A whimsical garden featuring hedges sculpted into elaborate animal and geometric shapes through the ancient art of topiary.
places.history: Developed as part of Chandigarh's horticultural showcase, featuring skilled topiary work that transforms ordinary hedges into living sculptures.
places.famousFor: Sculpted hedges, topiary art, and garden craft.

places.name: Hibiscus Garden
places.description: A colorful themed garden in the Leisure Valley showcasing dozens of hibiscus varieties in vibrant reds, pinks, yellows, and whites.
places.history: Part of the Leisure Valley chain of themed gardens, the Hibiscus Garden highlights the versatility of this tropical flower family with extensive collections.
places.famousFor: Hibiscus flower varieties, Leisure Valley garden chain.

places.name: Smriti Upvan
places.description: A memorial garden dedicated to preserving the memory of notable individuals and events in Chandigarh's history, featuring sculptures and commemorative installations.
places.history: Established as a civic memorial space, Smriti Upvan serves as a quiet tribute to the people and events that shaped Chandigarh's identity.
places.famousFor: Memorial garden, sculptures, and civic history.

places.name: Kala Gram
places.description: A cultural village and arts complex hosting regular exhibitions, craft fairs, and cultural festivals — a vibrant hub for regional art, music, and dance.
places.history: Built as a permanent cultural complex to promote North Indian arts and crafts. Hosts the annual Chandigarh Arts and Heritage Festival and other cultural events throughout the year.
places.famousFor: Arts and crafts exhibitions, cultural festivals, and regional heritage.

places.name: Fitness Trail Sector 10
places.description: A well-maintained jogging and fitness trail winding through the wooded green belt of Sector 10, with exercise stations, shaded pathways, and fresh mountain air.
places.history: Developed along Chandigarh's green belt network, this fitness trail is popular with morning joggers, walkers, and fitness enthusiasts in the museum district.
places.famousFor: Jogging track, outdoor fitness stations, and shaded walking paths.
